name: The Greyvale Anomaly
description: Reports of Hollow creatures far south of the Ashmark. A farming town frightened. An ancient ruin that's started glowing at night. Something is wrong in the Greyvale — and it's not a random incursion.

stages.name: The Road North
stages.objective: Travel to Millhaven and investigate the Hollow reports.
hints: Your companion mentions hearing that Millhaven is a day's travel north of the Accord.
Your companion suggests talking to Guildmaster Torin before leaving — he might have more details about what the scouts found.
Your companion says directly: 'Torin said the town's called Millhaven. Let's head north along the trade road.'

on_complete.narrative_beat: You arrive in Millhaven. It's quieter than a farming town should be.

stages.name: Something Wrong
stages.objective: Talk to Millhaven's residents and learn what they've seen.
hints: Your companion notices a woman watching you from near the mill — she looks like she's in charge.
Your companion suggests asking at the inn — 'Barkeeps always know what's going on.'
Your companion says: 'That's Elder Yanna. She runs this town. We should talk to her.'

on_complete.narrative_beat: The pieces are forming a picture. The creatures aren't random. The ruins are connected. You need to go there.

stages.name: First Blood
stages.objective: Engage the Hollow creatures near Millhaven and survive.
hints: Your companion's attention snaps north. 'Do you hear that? That... silence?'
Your companion draws their weapon. 'Something's moving out there. This is it.'
Your companion says: 'The tracks lead toward the northern fields. Ready up.'

on_complete.narrative_beat: The creatures are dead — or gone, or dispersed, you're not sure anything that wrong can really die. At the incursion site, you notice something. The residual energy doesn't match normal Hollow signatures. It's structured. Almost deliberate.

stages.name: What Lies Beneath
stages.objective: Explore the Greyvale Ruins and discover what's causing the disturbance.
hints: Your companion keeps glancing at the hills to the northeast. 'The elder mentioned the old ruins. And the lights.'
Your companion says: 'Whatever's happening with the Hollow, those ruins are connected. I'd bet on it.'
Your companion says directly: 'We need to go to the Greyvale Ruins. The answer's there.'

on_complete.narrative_beat: You hold the journal page. The notation is academic, precise. You can't read all of it, but some phrases are clear: 'resonance thinning,' 'controlled aperture,' 'acceptable threshold.' Someone was doing this on purpose. Your companion is very quiet.

keep_artifact.description: Keep the artifact. Don't show it to anyone yet.

show_companion.description: Show the artifact to your companion immediately.

stages.name: The Weight of Discovery
stages.objective: Return to the Accord of Tides and report what you found.
hints: Your companion says: 'We need to get this to someone who can read it. Back to the Accord.'
Your companion mentions the Aelindran scholars in the Dockside Quarter. 'If anyone can read old academic notation, it's them.'
Your companion says: 'Emris — the Aelindran archivist in the Dockside Quarter. They'd know what this means.'

on_complete.narrative_beat: Emris takes the journal page with trembling hands. They read it once. Then again. Their face goes through recognition, confusion, and something that looks a lot like fear. 'Where did you find this?' they ask, very quietly. 'Where exactly?'

player_death_in_ruins.consequence: respawn at Millhaven, ruins remain accessible, Hollow corruption +1

name: What Aldric Found
description: Aldric went to the ruins a month ago and came back wrong. He won't speak, barely eats, and traces strange shapes with his fingers. Elder Yanna has his stone but is afraid to show anyone. Something happened to him in those ruins, and finding out what might explain the Hollow's behavior in the Greyvale.

stages.name: The Man Who Came Back Wrong
stages.objective: Learn about Aldric from Millhaven's residents.
hints: Your companion notices the locals avoiding a topic. 'There's something they're not saying.'
Your companion suggests: 'The innkeeper seems to know everyone. Maybe ask about recent... problems.'
Your companion says: 'Maren mentioned someone named Aldric. And Elder Yanna keeps visiting someone. Same person?'

on_complete.narrative_beat: The picture forms: a man went north, came back, and brought something with him. Whatever happened in those ruins is still happening to Aldric.

stages.name: The Hollow Stare
stages.objective: Find Aldric and try to communicate with him.
hints: Your companion glances toward the corner booth. 'Is that... is he alright?'
Your companion says: 'He's not responding. But look at his hands — he's drawing something.'
Your companion says: 'Those shapes he's tracing. They look like the notation in the ruins.'

on_complete.narrative_beat: Aldric's eyes focus on you for one terrible second. 'The lights know you're looking,' he says. Then he's gone again, staring at nothing, fingers tracing symbols you've seen before.

stages.name: What He Brought Back
stages.objective: Convince Elder Yanna to show you Aldric's stone.
hints: Your companion says: 'Yanna's hiding something. She keeps touching her pocket.'
Your companion suggests: 'Maybe if she trusts us enough, she'll show us what Aldric brought back.'
Your companion says: 'Yanna, we've seen the ruins. Whatever Aldric found — we need to see it. To help him.'

on_complete.narrative_beat: Yanna reaches into her pocket with a shaking hand. The stone is warm. It hums. And when you hold it, for one vertiginous second, the world looks thin — as if you could see through it to something vast and dark beyond.

name: Millhaven Stands
description: The Hollow creatures are pressing closer to Millhaven. The town needs to prepare — barricades, watchtowers, evacuation routes. How the player allocates resources determines what happens when the attack comes.

stages.name: Taking Stock
stages.objective: Survey Millhaven's defenses and identify vulnerabilities.
hints: Your companion looks around the town. 'Not much to work with, is there?'
Your companion suggests walking the perimeter. 'Let's see what we've got to defend.'
Your companion says: 'The north side is completely open. If they come from the fields, there's nothing stopping them.'

on_complete.narrative_beat: The town is vulnerable from the north. Limited materials, limited hands. You'll have to choose where to focus.

stages.name: Hard Choices
stages.objective: Choose how to allocate Millhaven's resources: north barricade, watchtower, or evacuation supplies.
hints: Your companion lays out the options. 'Barricade to the north buys time. Watchtower gives warning. Evacuation supplies save lives if we lose.'
Your companion says: 'There's not enough for all three. Pick what matters most.'
Your companion says: 'What would you rather have — walls, eyes, or a way out?'

on_complete.narrative_beat: The townsfolk work through the night. By dawn, it's done — not much, but something. A line drawn in dirt and hope.

north_barricade.description: Build barricades on the north side to slow any attack.

watchtower.description: Build a watchtower for early warning.

evacuation_supplies.description: Prepare evacuation supplies and escape routes.

name: The Scholar's Burden
description: Emris has been studying artifacts from Aelindra for thirty years. The Greyvale discovery changes everything — the notation matches their missing mentor's work. Helping Emris may unlock deeper understanding of what happened at the ruins, and what Veythar was doing.

stages.name: Fragments and Echoes
stages.objective: Help Emris study the Greyvale artifacts.
hints: Your companion says: 'Emris looks like they haven't slept. Maybe we should check in.'
Your companion says: 'The scholar said they needed help comparing the notation to their archives.'
Your companion says: 'Emris is in their study on the dockside. They asked us to come back.'

on_complete.narrative_beat: Emris spreads their notes across every surface. 'Look — this notation. My mentor used this exact system. No one else did. This is personal scholarship. Private. Someone was working on something they didn't want the academy to know about.'

stages.name: The Last Letter
stages.objective: Earn Emris's trust enough for them to share their mentor's final letter.
hints: Your companion notices Emris holding something — a letter, old and well-handled. 'There's more they're not showing us.'
Your companion says: 'Be patient. They'll share when they're ready. Some knowledge costs trust.'
Your companion says: 'Emris, if your mentor's letter is connected to what we found... we need to see it. All of it.'

on_complete.narrative_beat: Emris unfolds the letter with trembling hands. The last words their mentor ever wrote: 'A discovery that changes everything. The barrier is not what we thought. It was made. And it can be unmade. I will write more when I understand.' They never wrote again.
